findings.description: The source unit bundles three distinct requirements: (1) attribution format '- [Name], LISRAYA patient', (2) italic/distinguished text styling, and (3) mandatory pairing with warm lifestyle imagery. The imagery-pairing requirement is a visual/assembly concern, not copy_editorial attribution styling, and should be split into its own rule so hardness/polarity/class can be independently governed.

new_rules.scope: patient quote attribution and text styling
patient quotes must be paired with warm lifestyle imagery

findings.description: Source unit cites a specific governance reference (§1.2) tying the footnote-marker requirement to a regulatory/legal source document. The rule lacks any governance facet (citation, source_ref, or compliance owner) capturing this traceability.

The rule is encoded only as polarity=must_not ('never reproduce in sections') but the source unit also asserts a positive placement requirement ('all live in the locked header/footer components'). This dual must/must_not structure is collapsed into a single must_not rule, losing the positive constraint that these elements are mandatorily required in the header/footer.

new_rules.scope: logo, copyright, ® legal line, job code, Indication, ISI must live in locked header/footer components
these elements must never be reproduced within body sections

findings.description: The elements listed (® legal line, job code, Indication, ISI) are core regulatory/legal compliance components, yet the rule carries no governance facet (e.g., regulatory_review_required, legal_owner) despite being a hard compliance boundary distinct from general layout rules.
